mission: Map TypeScript type conventions and patterns across the codebase
scope: All packages (opencode, core, console, desktop, function, app, sdks, extensions), all tsconfigs

strict_mode.verdict: All 12 tsconfig.json files use `strict: true`, which implies `useUnknownInCatchVariables: true` — unannotated catch variables default to `unknown`.

explicit_unknown.description: Only 3 explicit `catch (e: unknown)` annotations in the entire codebase. Extremely rare pattern — most developers rely on the `strict` default.
examples: packages/opencode/src/tool/propose-plan.ts:61 — `catch (e: unknown)` → re-wraps in `new Error(...)` with context

explicit_any_ANTIPATTERN.description: 14 instances of `catch (e: any)` — this is an anti-pattern under strict mode. Should be `unknown` with narrowing.
examples: packages/opencode/src/session/llm.ts:143 — `catch (e: any)` — LLM streaming error handler. Extracts `e.data?.text` — would benefit from `isRecord` narrowing

unannotated_defaults_to_unknown.description: 207 total catch clauses across the codebase. Most are unannotated `catch (e)` or `catch (error)` — these default to `unknown` under strict mode. This is the de facto convention, though not explicitly enforced by lint rules.
representative_samples: packages/opencode/src/storage/db.ts:32 — `catch { return defaultFlags }` — silent default pattern

post_catch_narrowing_patterns.example: packages/opencode/src/session/processor.ts:291 — `const isFilePart = (value: unknown): value is MessageV2.FilePart => Schema.is(MessageV2.FilePart)(value)`
packages/opencode/src/storage/db.ts:32 — `catch { return { disableChannelDb: false, skipMigrations: false } }`
packages/opencode/src/tool/propose-plan.ts:61 — `catch (e: unknown) { throw new Error(`claim_atoms is not valid...`) }`

bus_event_system.description: The bus/event system uses typed callbacks with `Payload<D>` for specific events and `any` for the wildcard subscribeAll path.
examples: packages/opencode/src/bus/index.ts:54 — `callback: (event: Payload<D>) => unknown` — typed callback with generic payload
packages/opencode/src/bus/index.ts:56 — `subscribeAllCallback: (callback: (event: any) => unknown) => Effect.Effect<() => void>` — wildcard callback uses `any` (debt)
packages/opencode/src/bus/index.ts:145 — `function on<T>(pubsub, type, callback: (event: T) => unknown)` — generic callback
packages/opencode/src/bus/index.ts:182 — `callback: (event: any) => unknown` — Effect.fn wildcard, uses `any`
packages/opencode/src/bus/index.ts:208 — `subscribe<D>(def, callback: (event: Payload<D>) => unknown)` — typed public API
packages/opencode/src/bus/index.ts:212 — `subscribeAll(callback: (event: any) => unknown)` — wildcard public API, uses `any`

storage_transaction.description: Database transaction callbacks use `TxOrDb` as the parameter type, but `Transaction` itself is typed as `any` (documented debt).
examples: packages/opencode/src/storage/db.ts:99 — `export function use<T>(callback: (trx: TxOrDb) => T): T` — LocalContext-wrapped transaction

provider_auth.description: Provider auth callbacks use Effect-based signatures with structured input.
examples: packages/opencode/src/provider/auth.ts:96 — `callback: (input: { providerID: ProviderID } & CallbackInput) => Effect.Effect<void, Error>`

pty.description: PTY event listeners use typed callbacks with specific event types.

cli_commands.description: CLI commands use `any` for handler args (debt).
examples: packages/opencode/src/cli/effect-cmd.ts:6 — `handler: (...args: any[]) => any; type WithArgs = any` — acknowledged with `builder?: any`

agent_lifecycle.description: Agent callbacks are simple void callbacks.
examples: packages/opencode/src/agent/agent.ts:449 — `onError: () => {}` — simple void callback with inline no-op default

general_pattern.return_types: Most callbacks return `void`, `unknown`, or `Effect.Effect<T, E, R>`. `unknown` is preferred over `void` for callbacks that may return a value that is intentionally discarded.
general_pattern.parameter_types: Callback parameters are almost always explicitly typed. Only test utilities, CLI handlers, and wildcard bus callbacks use `any`.
general_pattern.effect_callbacks: Effect-based callbacks (provider auth, bus internals) use `Effect.fn` or `Effect.gen` with proper `yield*` typing. The `EffectBridge` pattern bridges synchronous callbacks into the Effect runtime.

backend.opencode: Uses `pgTable()` from `drizzle-orm/pg-core` backed by PGlite (local) or node-postgres (remote). Schema files suffixed `.pg.sql.ts`.
backend.console: Uses `mysqlTable()` from drizzle-orm/mysql-core. Separate package, separate concern.
backend.duckdb: Has a parallel DuckDB analytics backend (`#db-duckdb` alias → `db.duckdb.ts`).

branded_types.description: Uses `.$type<BrandedType>()` on column definitions for semantic typing. This is the primary convention for type-safe IDs and domain types.
examples: packages/opencode/src/session/session.pg.sql.ts:43 — `model: jsonb().$type<{ id: string; providerID: string; variant?: string }>()` — inline type

type_inference.description: Two access patterns used for `$inferSelect` and `$inferInsert` — dot access (`.$inferSelect`) and bracket access (`['$inferSelect']`). Dot access is more common.
examples: packages/opencode/src/account/repo.ts:10 — `export type AccountRow = (typeof AccountTable)['$inferSelect']` — bracket access
packages/opencode/src/session/message-v2.ts:581 — `const info = (row: typeof MessageTable.$inferSelect) => ...` — inline function parameter type
packages/opencode/src/session/message-v2.ts:599 — `function hydrate(rows: (typeof MessageTable.$inferSelect)[])` — array of inferred rows
packages/opencode/src/control-plane/workspace.ts:65 — `function fromRow(row: typeof WorkspaceTable.$inferSelect): Info` — fromRow pattern

transaction_pattern.description: Custom transaction wrapper around LocalContext. The `Transaction` type is `any` (documented debt). Uses `TxOrDb = Transaction | Client` union.
examples: packages/opencode/src/storage/db.ts:57 — `export type Transaction = any` — DEPRECATED, has embedded comment acknowledging the debt
packages/opencode/src/storage/db.ts:136 — `(Client() as any).transaction(txCallback, ...)` — `as any` cast to bypass type issues

re_export.description: `storage/db.ts` re-exports all of `drizzle-orm` via `export * from 'drizzle-orm'`. 21 files import from this convenience path.

schema_aggregation.description: All pg table exports are aggregated in `storage/schema.ts` via re-exports.

timestamps_mixin.description: `schema.pg.sql.ts` exports a `TimestampsPg` object (with `time_created`, `time_updated`, `time_deleted` integer columns) that is spread into table definitions with `...TimestampsPg`.

vercel_ai_sdk.description: The Vercel AI SDK (`ai`) is the primary LLM abstraction. Types are imported both at runtime and as types.
examples: packages/opencode/src/session/llm.ts:6 — `streamText, wrapLanguageModel, type ModelMessage, type Tool` — mixed runtime/type import
packages/opencode/src/session/tools.ts:14 — `type Tool as AITool, tool, jsonSchema, type ToolExecutionOptions, asSchema` — renamed import to avoid naming conflict
packages/opencode/src/session/message-v2.ts:5 — `APICallError, convertToModelMessages, LoadAPIKeyError, type ModelMessage, type UIMessage` — both error classes and types
packages/opencode/src/provider/provider.ts:5 — `NoSuchModelError, type Provider as SDK` — error class + renamed type import
packages/opencode/src/provider/error.ts:1 — `import { APICallError } from 'ai'` — error class used as base

effect.description: Effect is the foundational runtime library. 510+ imports in opencode/src. Used pervasively for schema validation, service definitions, error handling, and concurrency.
patterns: Error: `Schema.TaggedErrorClass<MyError>()('Tag', { fields })` — 120 instances — the canonical error pattern in Effect
Schema: `Schema.Struct({...})`, `Schema.decodeUnknownOption(schema)`, `Schema.is(schema)(value)` — 15 uses of decodeUnknownOption, 1107+ Schema.Struct uses
Stream: `* as Stream from 'effect/Stream'` — path import convention

vscode.description: VSCode types are used only in the extension SDK (not in opencode core).
examples: sdks/vscode/src/extension.ts:4 — `import * as vscode from 'vscode'` — only vscode import in the entire repo

opencode_internal.description: Internal packages are imported through workspace references. Types are shared through the SDK and core packages.

unknown_to_typed_narrowing.name: isRecord
unknown_to_typed_narrowing.signature: export function isRecord(value: unknown): value is Record<string, unknown>
unknown_to_typed_narrowing.usage: Heavily used across 8+ files for narrowing unknown values to record objects after catch blocks, JSON parsing, or API responses

unknown_to_typed_narrowing.name: Schema.is
unknown_to_typed_narrowing.usage: Used 15+ times for Effect Schema-based runtime type guards
examples: packages/opencode/src/session/processor.ts:291 — `const isFilePart = (value: unknown): value is MessageV2.FilePart => Schema.is(MessageV2.FilePart)(value)`

unknown_to_typed_narrowing.name: Schema.decodeUnknownOption
unknown_to_typed_narrowing.usage: Safe JSON-to-typed decoding from unknown inputs. Used 15 times for config parsing, auth data, query params, manifest validation.

common_type_utilities.name: DeepPartial<T>
common_type_utilities.signature: export type DeepPartial<T> = T extends object ? { [K in keyof T]?: DeepPartial<T[K]> | null } : T

common_type_utilities.name: DeepMutable<T>
common_type_utilities.signature: export type DeepMutable<T> = T extends string | number | boolean | bigint | symbol | Function ...
common_type_utilities.usage: Wraps Schema.Schema.Type to strip readonly modifiers from Effect Schema-derived types

common_type_utilities.name: NotPromise<T>
common_type_utilities.signature: type NotPromise<T> = T extends Promise<any> ? never : T
common_type_utilities.usage: Enforces synchronous-only return values in transaction callbacks

common_type_utilities.name: NonNullable<T>
common_type_utilities.usage: 19+ instances. Primary pattern: extracting types from deeply nested optional/union types. Example: `packages/opencode/src/session/projectors-next.ts:14` — `NonNullable<(typeof SessionMessageTable.$inferInsert)['data']>`
common_type_utilities.convention: Widely used for unwrapping optional fields from API response types and config types

common_type_utilities.name: Schema.Schema.Type<typeof Schema>
common_type_utilities.location: Pattern used across session/, storage/, and tool/ modules
common_type_utilities.signature: Extracts the TypeScript type from an Effect Schema definition
common_type_utilities.usage: Standard pattern for deriving TS types from Effect Schemas. Example: `packages/opencode/src/session/message.ts:16` — `export type ToolCall = Schema.Schema.Type<typeof ToolCall>`

error_types.name: NamedError.create
error_types.usage: 29 instances. The canonical error factory for non-Effect errors.
examples: packages/opencode/src/storage/db.ts:19 — `export const NotFoundError = NamedError.create('NotFoundError', { message: Schema.String })`

error_types.name: Schema.TaggedErrorClass
error_types.usage: The canonical Effect error pattern. Each error has a string tag, can be caught with Effect.catchTag.

error_types.name: RetryReason union with (string & {})
error_types.usage: Discriminated union that allows literal matching while preserving autocomplete. The `(string & {})` is a TypeScript trick to allow any string while keeping specific literals in IDE autocomplete.

6_tsconfig_strictness.summary: All 12 tsconfig.json files use `'strict': true`. This is the universal convention.

explicit_overrides.noUncheckedIndexedAccess: false — explicitly set in packages/opencode/tsconfig.json (defaults to false under strict)
explicit_overrides.types: [] — explicitly empty in packages/opencode/tsconfig.json to avoid global type pollution
explicit_overrides.customConditions: ['browser'] — set in packages/opencode/tsconfig.json for bun runtime
explicit_overrides.jsx: 'preserve' with jsxImportSource '@opentui/solid' — in opencode, app, console packages

lint_rules.eslint: No ESLint TypeScript-specific rules found (no @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any, strict-boolean-expressions, or switch-exhaustiveness-check). The project uses `tsgo` (TypeScript native preview) for type checking rather than ESLint type-aware rules.
lint_rules.prettier: Listed in devDependencies of opencode package.

7_smoking_guns_and_debt.finding: Transaction = any is documented debt with a deprecation comment
7_smoking_guns_and_debt.impact: `TxOrDb` type depends on this. Type safety is lost throughout the entire database access layer. All transaction callbacks receive untyped transaction objects.
7_smoking_guns_and_debt.note: Comment suggests replacing with `PgliteTransaction | NodePgTransaction` union when the audit arrives.

7_smoking_guns_and_debt.impact: Loses type safety on error objects. These should use `unknown` with narrowing (isRecord, Schema.is, or instanceof).
Wildcard subscribers lose type safety. The wildcard bus is the primary mechanism for global event observation.

7_smoking_guns_and_debt.finding: CLI command builder uses `any` for handler args
7_smoking_guns_and_debt.impact: Type safety is lost at the CLI boundary. Commands that use this builder receive untyped arguments.

7_smoking_guns_and_debt.finding: No lint rule enforces `unknown` in catch clauses or bans `any`
7_smoking_guns_and_debt.impact: The codebase relies on developer discipline and `strict: true` defaults. Adding `@typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any` and `@typescript-eslint/use-unknown-in-catch-callback-variable` rules would provide guardrails.

7_smoking_guns_and_debt.finding: Only 3 explicit `catch (e: unknown)` annotations exist — the codebase relies on the strict default
7_smoking_guns_and_debt.impact: While technically correct (strict mode makes unannotated catch variables `unknown`), explicit annotations would improve readability and make the intent clearer to developers not familiar with the strict mode behavior.
Array and object index access does not include `| undefined` in the type. Common in codebases that use `NonNullable` extensively to work around optional types.

8_convention_verdicts.catch_clauses: Convention is unannotated `catch (e)` relying on strict mode to make it `unknown`. Explicit `unknown` annotations are rare (3 instances). Explicit `any` annotations are debt (14 instances). Post-catch narrowing uses instanceof, isRecord(), Schema.is(), or silent swallowing with defaults.
8_convention_verdicts.callbacks: Callbacks are almost always explicitly typed with their parameter types. `unknown` is preferred over `void` for return types (signals 'value intentionally ignored'). `any` appears only in wildcard bus subscriptions, CLI handlers, and test utilities.
8_convention_verdicts.drizzle: pgTable() with .$type<>() for branded IDs. Dot-access `.$inferSelect` is the convention. Schema aggregation via re-export barrel files. Transaction type is acknowledged debt (typed as `any`).
8_convention_verdicts.third_party: Vercel AI SDK types imported with `type` keyword. Effect Schema used as canonical schema layer. vscode types isolated to extension SDK. Renamed imports used to avoid naming conflicts (e.g., `type Tool as AITool`).
8_convention_verdicts.utility_types: `isRecord` is the primary unknown-to-Record narrowing. `DeepMutable` strips readonly from Schema-derived types. `NonNullable` used extensively for unwrapping optional fields. Effect `Schema.is` and `Schema.decodeUnknownOption` are the preferred runtime validation patterns for unknown inputs.
